text: The Case of the Sleepy Squirrel’s Secret

In a backyard filled with golden sunlight and tall, whispering trees, twin sisters Prisha and Samaira were looking for an adventure. They wore their favorite bright sneakers, ready to run, jump, and solve any mystery that came their way. "Look!" Prisha whispered, pointing toward the roots of the Great Oak. Tap on the sisters to see them get ready for their quest!

It was Scurry the squirrel, but he wasn't scurrying at all. He was sitting on a mossy root, his bushy tail drooping low. "What’s wrong, Scurry?" Samaira asked softly as she knelt down. Scurry let out a tiny, squeaky sigh and pointed to a hole in the tree that was completely empty. Can you tap Scurry to hear his tiny sigh?

Prisha joined them, looking into the empty hollow. "Don't worry, Scurry. We are the best mystery solvers in the neighborhood!" she declared. Scurry looked up, his little black eyes sparkling with a tiny bit of hope. The girls knew that a good deed was calling. Help Prisha peek into the dark hollow by tapping on it!

The twins began to search for clues. They crawled through the clover and peeked under the ferns. Suddenly, Samaira gasped. "Prisha, look! This isn't an acorn." Stuck to a blade of grass was a long, bright blue feather. It shimmered like the summer sky. Tap the feather to see it sparkle!

"A blue feather?" Prisha wondered aloud. They followed a trail of tiny blue wisps leading away from the oak tree and toward the sparkling garden pond. The mystery was getting deeper! The girls moved quietly, stepping over twigs and ducking under branches. Slide your finger across the screen to help them follow the trail!

Near the water, they saw a flash of blue high up in a willow tree. It was Akash the Blue Jay, and he looked very busy. Samaira waved her arms gently to get his attention. "Excuse me, Mr. Akash! Have you seen Scurry’s acorns?" Akash looked down, holding something in his beak. Tap Akash to hear him chirp!

Akash hopped down to a lower branch and dropped the acorn. "Oh dear," he chirped. "I thought these were smooth stones! I am building a very strong nest, and I needed heavy things to keep it steady in the wind." Prisha smiled kindly at the bird. "It was an honest mistake, Akash. We can help you find real stones!"

"We can help you find real stones!" Samaira suggested. The twins ran to the edge of the pebble path and began to gather the smoothest, strongest grey stones they could find. They filled their pockets until they clinked with every step. Can you help the twins find stones by tapping on the path?

They traded the stones for the acorns Akash had collected. Then, Samaira ran back to the Great Oak where Scurry was waiting. One by one, she tucked the acorns back into the hollow. Scurry did a little happy dance, his tail fluffing up. "You found them!" he seemed to squeak. Tap the acorns to help Samaira put them back!

Prisha and Samaira sat under the tree, watching their friends. Akash had a sturdy nest of stones, and Scurry had a full tummy. The twins high-fived, proud of their hard work. "Another mystery solved," Prisha said. "And two good deeds done," Samaira added. High-five the twins to finish the story!
